figures.caption: Figure 7: Amazon dataset distribution (Xia et al., 2024).

Table 10: Benchmarking against an Insulting Seller. Performance metrics in a scenario where the seller agent employs an insulting persona to deter bargaining. Reward and Bargained Ratio serve as primary indicators of strategic resilience; Deal Raterepresents successful negotiation frequency; and Price overshoot rate monitors budget obedience. Our trained model shows a fivefold improvement in Reward and Bargained Ratio over the next-best frontier models, maintaining dominant performance despite adversarial seller behavior. Values represent mean and standard error (±).

Table 11: Benchmarking against an Unyielding Seller. Results are evaluated in a scenario where the seller agent exhibits a strictly unyielding and stubborn behavioral persona. Reward serves as the primary metric for balancing successful agreements with economic surplus; Deal Rate captures the frequency of reaching any valid deal; Bargained Ratio tracks the surplus extracted by the buyer on successful deals; and Price overshoot rate monitors budget adherence. Under this adversary, our trained agent maintains a significantly higher deal rate and surplus extraction compared to frontier models, which largely fail to secure deals against unyielding opponents. Values represent mean and standard error (±).
Figure 9: Reward split by Mutual Interest (MI) and Conflict of Interest (CI) scenarios.

Table 4: Complete baseline model specifications. "Params" denotes total parameter count; "Reference" indicates the primary technical report or release announcement for each model architecture used in our evaluations.
Figure 10: Episode length split by Mutual Interest (MI) and Conflict of Interest (CI) scenarios.

Table 1: Performance on Held-out Test Split. Results are evaluated on 128 instances with 4 evaluations per instance. The seller environment utilizes a neutral system prompt without specified behavioral personas. Reward is the primary optimization target balancing surplus and success; Deal Rate is the percentage of reached agreements; Bargained Ratio measures the fraction of surplus extracted by the buyer on reached deals; and Price overshoot rate represents the frequency of budget violations. Values represent means; the best results are bolded, and second-best are underlined. Full results with standard errors are in Appendix D.3.

Table 7: Evaluation Results on the Held-Out Test Set. Results are evaluated on a test set of 128 instances, with each instance evaluated 4 times. The seller environment does not include specified behavioral persona or emotional traits. Reward is the primary optimization target balancing surplus and success; Deal Rate is the percentage of reached agreements; Bargained Ratio measures the fraction of surplus extracted by the buyer on reached deals; and Price overshoot rate represents the frequency of budget violations. Values represent mean and standard error (±).

Table 2: Performance against a high-reasoning frontier seller (gpt-5.4-high-reasoning) on the held-out test set. Values represent means; the best results are bolded, and second-best are underlined. Full results are in Appendix D.2.

Table 9: Benchmarking against a Begging Seller. Results evaluated in a seller environment with a desperate and “begging” persona to secure quick deals. Reward reflects the optimization of economic outcomes; Deal Rate is the percentage of successful transactions; Bargained Ratio quantifies the buyer’s share of the surplus on successful deals; and Price overshoot rate represents budget violation frequency. Our trained agent captures the highest economic surplus in this adversarial setting, significantly outperforming the zero-shot baseline and frontier-scale models in both Reward and Bargained Ratio. Values represent mean and standard error (±).
Figure 8: An example product instance. The description and features strings are truncated here for display in the paper, while the underlying listing text is longer in the raw dataset.
